title: LTI 1.3 & LTI Advantage: launching tools from the LMS
summary: When a student clicks an external tool inside Canvas or Moodle, LTI is what makes it work securely. Today: how LTI 1.3 replaced the old OAuth signing with OIDC + JWTs, and the three LTI Advantage services that share rosters and grades.

activities.title: What LTI is, and why 1.3 matters

activities.question: LTI 1.3 secures tool launches using which technologies (replacing OAuth 1.0a signing)?
options: API keys placed in the URL
activities.explanation: LTI 1.3 uses the OIDC third-party-initiated login, signed JWTs to carry launch claims, and OAuth 2.0 for service calls — a big security upgrade over LTI 1.1's OAuth 1.0a signing.

activities.question: Which three services make up LTI Advantage?
options: Deep Linking, Names & Role Provisioning (NRPS), Assignment & Grade (AGS)
activities.explanation: LTI Advantage = Deep Linking 2.0 + NRPS 2.0 + AGS 2.0 on top of LTI Core 1.3. (Watch the trap: 'Rostering, Gradebook, Resource' is OneRoster 1.2, a different standard.)

activities.question: What does Names & Role Provisioning Services (NRPS) give an LTI tool?
activities.explanation: NRPS securely shares the roster/enrollment of the launching context with the tool, so it knows who is in the course.

activities.title: LTI 1.3 resource-link launch
activities.mermaid:
flowchart TD
  A[User clicks the LTI link] --> B[Platform sends OIDC login init]
  B --> C[Tool returns auth request]
  C --> D[Platform posts signed id_token JWT]
  D --> E[Tool validates the JWT]
  E --> F[Tool renders the resource]

steps.title: Click the link
steps.caption: A user clicks an external tool link inside the LMS.

steps.title: OIDC login init
steps.caption: The platform starts the OpenID Connect third-party login.

steps.title: Tool auth request
steps.caption: The tool responds with an authentication request.

steps.title: Signed id_token
steps.caption: The platform POSTs a signed JWT carrying the launch claims.

steps.title: Validate JWT
steps.caption: The tool verifies the JWT signature and claims.

steps.title: Render
steps.caption: The tool renders the resource for the user.

activities.caption: Every secure LTI 1.3 launch is an OIDC handshake carrying a signed JWT — no shared secrets in the URL.

activities.headline: Moodle 5.1 certified for LTI Assignment & Grade Services 2.0
activities.summary: On **April 7, 2026**, 1EdTech's certification registry recorded **Moodle 5.1** as certified for **LTI AGS 2.0** — the LTI Advantage service that writes grades from external tools back into the LMS gradebook. Certification matters: it's how institutions trust that two products will actually interoperate.

items: **LTI 1.3** (2019) swapped OAuth 1.0a for **OIDC + signed JWT + OAuth 2.0**.
**LTI Advantage** = **Deep Linking + NRPS + AGS** — content, rosters, grades.
Don't confuse LTI Advantage's three services with **OneRoster's** Rostering/Gradebook/Resource.
**Certification** (e.g., Moodle 5.1 + AGS) is the real interoperability guarantee.

glossary.definition: Learning Tools Interoperability (1EdTech) — standard for launching external tools/content from an LMS; v1.3 uses OIDC + signed JWTs.
Assignment & Grade Services 2.0 — the LTI Advantage service that syncs grades from a tool into the LMS gradebook.
Names & Role Provisioning Services 2.0 — LTI Advantage service that shares a course's roster/enrollments with a tool.
LTI Advantage service for selecting tool content from within the LMS and embedding it.

reflection: If ManageArk acts as an LTI platform (or tool), which Advantage service would you implement first — NRPS to share rosters, or AGS to collect grades — and how does that compare to going OneRoster instead?
